[[Corporal/Legends|Corporal]] '''Derdram''' was a member of the [[Internal Security Branch]] in [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Also serving in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] by the same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Derdram was assigned to the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] with the responsibility of ensuring the physical safety of Imperial personnel from [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]].

==Biography==
Derdram was a [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CCG" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="junior novel" /> who served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]]<ref name="ESB" /> by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="TNEC" /> He had joined [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] and was assigned to the organization's [[Internal Security Branch]]<ref name="CCG" /> by 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]]. By then,<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|V}} to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> he<ref name="CSWE" /> had become a [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]], and was assigned to the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']],<ref name="CCG" /> an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] which served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="ESB" /> Derdram's role aboard the ''Executor'' was to ensure the physical safety of Imperial personnel from [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]].<ref name="CCG" />

[[File:MKae_crew_pit.png|thumb|left|250px|Derdram aboard the crew pit of the ''Avenger'']]
In 3 ABY,<ref name="TNEC" /> following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Derdram was stationed in the crew pits of the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|command bridge]] aboard another Imperial [[Starship/Legends|starship]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Lorth Needa/Legends|Lorth Needa's]] [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']], part of Vader's personal [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]]. After the Battle of Hoth, the ''Avenger'' pursued the fugitive [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] through the [[Hoth system/Legends|Hoth system]]. At one point during the pursuit, the ''Falcon'' caused the ''Avenger'' to almost collide with another [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]]. The ''Avenger'' chased the ''Falcon'' into the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]],<ref name="ESB" /> and suffered significant damage when it traveled into the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] to search for the ''Falcon''.<ref name="GG3">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> When the ''Avenger'' pursued the fugitive ''Falcon'' into the neighboring [[Anoat system/Legends|Anoat system]], the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] charged the bridge, causing Derdram and others aboard the crew pits to dive for cover<ref name="ESB" /> and setting off alarms throughout the ''Avenger''.<ref name="ESB novel">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]]</ref>

With the ''Millennium Falcon'' no longer detected by the ''Avenger''{{'s}} [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], unbeknownst to Derdram and the others aboard the ''Avenger'', the freighter had simply landed on a blind spot of the Star Destroyer's conning tower. When Darth Vader demanded an update on the pursuit of the ''Falcon'', Needa decided to personally apologize to Vader for the evident loss of the ''Falcon'', taking a [[TIE/sh VIP shuttle]] to the ''Executor'' and being [[Death/Legends|executed]] by Vader for the failure. The Star Destroyers of Death Squadron ultimately dispersed into hyperspace in search of the ''Falcon'', not knowing the freighter was still hiding on the ''Avenger''{{'s}} conning tower. The ''Avenger'' prepared to go to hyperspace in the opposite direction of the rest of the fleet, emptying its garbage into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] beforehand. The ''Falcon'' took the opportunity to escape by floating alongside the garbage, evading the Imperial fleet.<ref name="ESB" />

==Personality and traits==
Derdram was fair-skinned with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="ESB" />

==Equipment==
During his Imperial service, Derdram wore a black [[Imperial Navy crewman]]'s [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform]].<ref name="ESB" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:NemetsClone.png|thumb|right|300px|Ralph Morse and other ''Avenger'' extras seen on the ''Executor'' in reused footage]]
Derdram first appeared in the [[1980]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']],<ref name="ESB" /> in footage shot on Stage 5 at [[Elstree Studios]] at some point between [[April 24]] and [[May 2]], [[1979]].<ref name="365Days">''[[Creating the Worlds of Star Wars: 365 Days]]''</ref> While not credited in the film,<ref name="ESB" /> [[Ralph Morse]] recalled playing the character.<ref name="Morse">{{WebCite|url=http://ralphmorse.com/Star_Wars.html|text=An interview with Morse|archivedate=20111203172349}}</ref> Towards the end of the film, Ralph Morse and other extras from the ''Avenger'' are visible on the ''Executor'' when footage of the former Star Destroyer was reused for a later scene.<ref name="ESB" />

The Derdram character would be named on a card in the ''[[Dagobah Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] in [[1997]]. While the lore establishes Derdram's role aboard the ''Executor'', the image is a still of Ralph Morse from the ''Avenger'' scenes of ''The Empire Strikes Back''. Because of the card using an image from the ''Avenger'' scenes to represent Derdram,<ref name="CCG" /> this article only assumes Derdram is the character aboard the ''Avenger'', and avoids making a clear link to the character on the ''Executor''.
